title: EndoSysScore: phenotype-aware risk estimation for under-recognised high-risk subgroups in surgical infective endocarditis
description: Reproducibility package for EndoSysScore (ESS), a phenotype-aware clinical prediction model for 30-day mortality after cardiac surgery for infective endocarditis, developed on the GIROC multicentre Italian registry. Development (n=4,285) and validation (n=930) cohorts were drawn from the same 23-centre GIROC registry; validation was a stratified random patient-level hold-out (by centre and year): all 23 centres contributed to both cohorts, with no patient overlap. The deposited results therefore correspond to held-out internal (split-sample) validation of overall discrimination and calibration. Because the six target phenotypes are individually rare, their generalisation was assessed by pre-specified, scenario-based phenotype validation with blinded synthetic-realism testing (TIMA-3), rather than by a conventional external cohort. Fully external validation (leave-one-centre-out and prospective inter-registry evaluation) is the planned next step. Headline results (held-out internal validation, n=930): AUC 0.785 (95% CI 0.74-0.83); O/E 1.05; Brier 0.09; calibration slope 0.88, intercept -0.14; Hosmer-Lemeshow p=0.26; DeLong superiority 6/6 vs comparators. Across six rare high-lethality phenotypes (observed 30-day mortality 50-55%), conventional scores underestimated mortality by 12-42 percentage points; ESS reduced the mean absolute observed-to-predicted deviation to 3.4 pp (RISK-E 15.7 pp). TIMA-3 synthetic realism: pooled expert accuracy 49.5% (95% CI 46.4-52.6), Cohen kappa -0.01, binomial p=0.776. Contents: de-identified development and validation datasets, full registry (n=5,215, descriptive), trained model and feature definitions, model/comparator predictions, global and phenotype-level results, DeLong and decision-curve outputs, TIMA-3 raw responses and statistics, the training pipeline, and the standalone pure-JS web calculator. Reported per TRIPOD+AI. Code: MIT licence. Calculator: https://endosysscore.org . Source: https://github.com/sandro2462/sys-score . Cite this deposit by its concept DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.20327974 .

creators.name: Italian Group of Research for Outcome in Cardiac Surgery (GIROC)
